title: lichen_sha256 output parameter not bounds-checked
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_util.c:11-28, lichen_util.h:69-70

lichen_sha256() signature declares output as uint8_t output[TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE] but this is just syntactic sugar - it decays to uint8_t*. The function does not verify the caller provided a buffer of at least TC_SHA256_DIGEST_SIZE bytes.

What goes wrong: A caller passing a smaller buffer would have a buffer overflow when tc_sha256_final writes 32 bytes. The array parameter syntax provides false safety assurance.

Options:
1. Document clearly that caller MUST provide 32 bytes
2. Add a size parameter and check it
3. Use a struct wrapper to enforce size at compile time

Current callers (ipv6_addr.c, lora_l2.c) all use correctly-sized buffers, so no immediate bug.
close_reason: Fixed: Added explicit SECURITY documentation warning that output buffer must be >= 32 bytes and that the array parameter syntax provides no compile-time bounds checking. Runtime bounds checking was not added because: (1) all current callers use correctly-sized buffers, (2) adding a size parameter would change the API with no practical benefit, and (3) a struct wrapper would be over-engineering. The documentation now clearly warns callers about the requirement.
